content.thinking:
Now let me understand the user's request better. They said:

> "we need to make it more clear that --local only affects the settings.json for entire, not for the agent"

So the issue is about the `--local` flag on `entire enable`. The user wants to clarify in the help text / documentation that `--local` only affects the Entire settings file (writing to `settings.local.json` instead of `settings.json`), but it doesn't affect the agent's settings (e.g., `.claude/settings.json` vs `.claude/settings.local.json`).

Let me explore the codebase to understand the current `--local` flag implementation and help text.

   221→// runEnableWithStrategy enables Entire with a specified strategy (non-interactive).
   222→// The selectedStrategy can be either a display name (manual-commit, auto-commit)
   223→// or an internal name (manual-commit, auto-commit).
   224→func runEnableWithStrategy(w io.Writer, selectedStrategy string, localDev, _, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   225→	// Map the strategy to internal name if it's a display name
   226→	internalStrategy := selectedStrategy
   227→	if mapped, ok := strategyDisplayToInternal[selectedStrategy]; ok {
   228→		internalStrategy = mapped
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	// Validate the strategy exists
   232→	strat, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy)
   233→	if err != nil {
   234→		return fmt.Errorf("unknown strategy: %s (use manual-commit or auto-commit)", selectedStrategy)
   235→	}
   236→
   237→	// Detect default agent
   238→	ag := agent.Default()
   239→	agentType := string(agent.AgentTypeClaudeCode)
   240→	if ag != nil {
   241→		agentType = string(ag.Type())
   242→	}
   243→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s (use --agent to change)\n\n", agentType)
   244→
   245→	// Setup Claude Code hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   246→	if _, err := setupClaudeCodeHook(localDev, forceHooks); err != nil {
   247→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup Claude Code hooks: %w", err)
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	// Setup .entire directory
   251→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   252→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   253→	}
   254→
   255→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   256→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   257→	if err != nil {
   258→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   259→		settings = &EntireSettings{}
   260→	}
   261→	// Update the specific fields
   262→	settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   263→	settings.LocalDev = localDev
   264→	settings.Enabled = true
   265→
   266→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   267→	if skipPushSessions {
   268→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   269→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   270→		}
   271→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   272→	}
   273→
   274→	// Handle telemetry for non-interactive mode
   275→	// Note: if telemetry is nil (not configured), it defaults to disabled
   276→	if !telemetry || os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   277→		f := false
   278→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	// Determine which settings file to write to
   282→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   283→	if err != nil {
   284→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   285→	}
   286→	shouldUseLocal, showNotification := determineSettingsTarget(entireDirAbs, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings)
   287→
   288→	if showNotification {
   289→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Info: Project settings exist. Saving to settings.local.json instead.")
   290→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Use --project to update the project settings file.")
   291→	}
   292→
   293→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   294→	if shouldUseLocal {
   295→		if err := SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings); err != nil {
   296→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save local settings: %w", err)
   297→		}
   298→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   299→	} else {
   300→		if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   301→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   302→		}
   303→	}
   304→
   305→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   306→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   307→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   308→	}
   309→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "✓ Hooks installed")
   310→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplay)
   311→
   312→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements
   313→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   314→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   315→	}
   316→
   317→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   318→
   319→	return nil
   320→}

   322→// runEnableInteractive runs the interactive enable flow.
   323→func runEnableInteractive(w io.Writer, localDev, _, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   324→	// Check if already fully enabled — show summary and return early.
   325→	// Skip early return if any configuration flags are set (user wants to reconfigure).
   326→	hasConfigFlags := forceHooks || skipPushSessions || !telemetry || useLocalSettings || useProjectSettings || localDev
   327→	if !hasConfigFlags {
   328→		if fullyEnabled, agentDesc, configPath := isFullyEnabled(); fullyEnabled {
   329→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "Already enabled. Everything looks good.")
   330→			fmt.Fprintln(w)
   331→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Agent: %s\n", agentDesc)
   332→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Config: %s\n", configPath)
   333→			return nil
   334→		}
   335→	}
   336→
   337→	// Detect default agent
   338→	ag := agent.Default()
   339→	agentType := string(agent.AgentTypeClaudeCode)
   340→	if ag != nil {
   341→		agentType = string(ag.Type())
   342→	}
   343→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s (use --agent to change)\n\n", agentType)
   344→
   345→	// Setup Claude Code hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   346→	if _, err := setupClaudeCodeHook(localDev, forceHooks); err != nil {
   347→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup Claude Code hooks: %w", err)
   348→	}
   349→
   350→	// Setup .entire directory
   351→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   352→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   353→	}
   354→
   355→	// Use the default strategy (manual-commit)
   356→	internalStrategy := strategy.DefaultStrategyName
   357→
   358→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   359→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   362→		settings = &EntireSettings{}
   363→	}
   364→	// Update the specific fields
   365→	settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   366→	settings.LocalDev = localDev
   367→	settings.Enabled = true
   368→
   369→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   370→	if skipPushSessions {
   371→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   372→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   373→		}
   374→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   375→	}
   376→
   377→	// Determine which settings file to write to
   378→	// First run always creates settings.json (no prompt)
   379→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   380→	if err != nil {
   381→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   382→	}
   383→	shouldUseLocal, showNotification := determineSettingsTarget(entireDirAbs, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings)
   384→
   385→	if showNotification {
   386→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Info: Project settings exist. Saving to settings.local.json instead.")
   387→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Use --project to update the project settings file.")
   388→	}
   389→
   390→	// Helper to save settings to the appropriate file
   391→	saveSettings := func() error {
   392→		if shouldUseLocal {
   393→			return SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings)
   394→		}
   395→		return SaveEntireSettings(settings)
   396→	}
   397→
   398→	// Save settings before telemetry prompt so config is persisted even if the user cancels
   399→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   400→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   401→	}
   402→
   403→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   404→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   405→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   406→	}
   407→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "✓ Hooks installed")
   408→
   409→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   410→	if shouldUseLocal {
   411→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   412→	}
   413→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplay)
   414→
   415→	// Ask about telemetry consent (only if not already asked)
   416→	fmt.Fprintln(w)
   417→	if err := promptTelemetryConsent(settings, telemetry); err != nil {
   418→		return fmt.Errorf("telemetry consent: %w", err)
   419→	}
   420→	// Save again to persist telemetry choice
   421→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   422→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   423→	}
   424→
   425→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements
   426→	strat, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy)
   427→	if err != nil {
   428→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   429→	}
   430→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   431→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   432→	}
   433→
   434→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   435→
   436→	return nil
   437→}

   482→// checkDisabledGuard checks if Entire is disabled and prints a message if so.
   483→// Returns true if the caller should exit (i.e., Entire is disabled).
   484→// On error reading settings, defaults to enabled (returns false).
   485→func checkDisabledGuard(w io.Writer) bool {
   486→	enabled, err := IsEnabled()
   487→	if err != nil {

   489→		return false
   490→	}
   491→	if !enabled {
   492→		fmt.Fprintln(w, DisabledMessage)
   493→		return true
   494→	}
   495→	return false

   546→// setupAgentHooksNonInteractive sets up hooks for a specific agent non-interactively.
   547→// If strategyName is provided, it sets the strategy; otherwise uses default.
   548→func setupAgentHooksNonInteractive(w io.Writer, ag agent.Agent, strategyName string, localDev, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   549→	agentName := ag.Name()
   550→	// Check if agent supports hooks
   551→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
   552→	if !ok {
   553→		return fmt.Errorf("agent %s does not support hooks", agentName)
   554→	}
   555→
   556→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s\n\n", ag.Type())
   557→
   558→	// Install agent hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   559→	installedHooks, err := hookAgent.InstallHooks(localDev, forceHooks)
   560→	if err != nil {
   561→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install hooks for %s: %w", agentName, err)
   562→	}
   563→
   564→	// Setup .entire directory
   565→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   566→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   567→	}
   568→
   569→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   570→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   571→	if err != nil {
   572→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   573→		settings = &EntireSettings{Strategy: strategy.DefaultStrategyName}
   574→	}
   575→	settings.Enabled = true
   576→	if localDev {
   577→		settings.LocalDev = localDev
   578→	}
   579→
   580→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   581→	if skipPushSessions {
   582→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   583→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   584→		}
   585→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   586→	}
   587→
   588→	// Set strategy if provided
   589→	if strategyName != "" {
   590→		// Map display name to internal name if needed
   591→		internalStrategy := strategyName
   592→		if mapped, ok := strategyDisplayToInternal[strategyName]; ok {
   593→			internalStrategy = mapped
   594→		}
   595→		// Validate the strategy exists
   596→		if _, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy); err != nil {
   597→			return fmt.Errorf("unknown strategy: %s (use manual-commit or auto-commit)", strategyName)
   598→		}
   599→		settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   600→	}
   601→
   602→	// Handle telemetry for non-interactive mode
   603→	// Note: if telemetry is nil (not configured), it defaults to disabled
   604→	if !telemetry || os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   605→		f := false
   606→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   607→	}
   608→
   609→	if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   610→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   611→	}
   612→
   613→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   614→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   615→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   616→	}
   617→
   618→	if installedHooks == 0 {
   619→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Hooks for %s already installed", ag.Description())
   620→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   621→			msg += " (Preview)"
   622→		}
   623→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   624→	} else {
   625→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Installed %d hooks for %s", installedHooks, ag.Description())
   626→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   627→			msg += " (Preview)"
   628→		}
   629→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   630→	}
   631→
   632→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplayProject)
   633→
   634→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements (creates entire/checkpoints/v1 branch, etc.)
   635→	strat, err := strategy.Get(settings.Strategy)
   636→	if err != nil {
   637→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   638→	}
   639→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   640→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   641→	}
   642→
   643→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   644→
   645→	return nil
   646→}

   656→// determineSettingsTarget decides whether to write to settings.local.json based on:
   657→// - Whether settings.json already exists
   658→// - The --local and --project flags
   659→// Returns (useLocal, showNotification).
   660→func determineSettingsTarget(entireDir string, useLocal, useProject bool) (bool, bool) {
   661→	// Explicit --local flag always uses local settings
   662→	if useLocal {
   663→		return true, false
   664→	}
   665→
   666→	// Explicit --project flag always uses project settings
   667→	if useProject {
   668→		return false, false
   669→	}
   670→
   671→	// No flags specified - check if settings file exists
   672→	settingsPath := filepath.Join(entireDir, paths.SettingsFileName)
   673→	if _, err := os.Stat(settingsPath); err == nil {
   674→		// Settings file exists - auto-redirect to local with notification
   675→		return true, true
   676→	}
   677→
   678→	// Settings file doesn't exist - create it
   679→	return false, false
   680→}

   682→// setupEntireDirectory creates the .entire directory and gitignore.
   683→// Returns true if the directory was created, false if it already existed.
   684→func setupEntireDirectory() (bool, error) { //nolint:unparam // already present in codebase
   685→	// Get absolute path for the .entire directory
   686→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   687→	if err != nil {
   688→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   689→	}
   690→
   691→	// Check if directory already exists
   692→	created := false
   693→	if _, err := os.Stat(entireDirAbs); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   694→		created = true
   695→	}
   696→
   697→	// Create .entire directory
   698→	//nolint:gosec // G301: Project directory needs standard permissions for git
   699→	if err := os.MkdirAll(entireDirAbs, 0o755); err != nil {
   700→		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to create .entire directory: %w", err)
   701→	}
   702→
   703→	// Create/update .gitignore with all required entries
   704→	if err := strategy.EnsureEntireGitignore(); err != nil {
   705→		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .gitignore: %w", err)
   706→	}
   707→
   708→	return created, nil
   709→}

   751→// shellCompletionComment is the comment preceding the completion line
   752→const shellCompletionComment = "# Entire CLI shell completion"

   754→// errUnsupportedShell is returned when the user's shell is not supported for completion.
   755→var errUnsupportedShell = errors.New("unsupported shell")
   756→
   757→// shellCompletionTarget returns the rc file path and completion lines for the
   758→// user's current shell.
   759→func shellCompletionTarget() (shellName, rcFile, completionLine string, err error) {
   760→	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   761→	if err != nil {
   762→		return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot determine home directory: %w", err)
   763→	}
   764→
   765→	shell := os.Getenv("SHELL")
   766→	switch {
   767→	case strings.Contains(shell, "zsh"):
   768→		return "Zsh",
   769→			filepath.Join(home, ".zshrc"),
   770→			"autoload -Uz compinit && compinit && source <(entire completion zsh)",
   771→			nil
   772→	case strings.Contains(shell, "bash"):
   773→		bashRC := filepath.Join(home, ".bashrc")
   774→		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")); err == nil {
   775→			bashRC = filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")
   776→		}
   777→		return "Bash",
   778→			bashRC,
   779→			"source <(entire completion bash)",
   780→			nil
   781→	case strings.Contains(shell, "fish"):
   782→		return "Fish",
   783→			filepath.Join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish"),
   784→			"entire completion fish | source",
   785→			nil
   786→	default:
   787→		return "", "", "", errUnsupportedShell
   788→	}
   789→}

## Summary
- Updated `--local` and `--project` flag descriptions on `enable` and `disable` commands to include the `.entire/` prefix, making it clear these flags affect Entire's own settings files, not the agent's settings (e.g., `.claude/settings.json`)

Fixes #279
Fixes #293

- [ ] Run `entire enable --help` and verify `--local` and `--project` descriptions mention `.entire/` paths
- [ ] Run `entire disable --help` and verify `--project` description mentions `.entire/` paths
